# read ISO9660 volume descriptor / El Torito to understand structure without mount
f=open('/app/alpine.iso','rb')
f.seek(32769)
print('magic', f.read(5))
# read primary volume descriptor volume id
f.seek(0x8028)
print('volid', f.read(32))
EOF
which 7z isoinfo xorriso bsdtar 2>&1

edits.new:
#!/bin/sh
exec qemu-system-x86_64 \
  -m 2048 -smp 4 \
  -kernel /tmp/ex/boot/vmlinuz-lts \
  -initrd /tmp/ex/boot/initramfs-lts \
  -append "modules=loop,squashfs,sd-mod,usb-storage console=ttyS0" \
  -drive file=/app/alpine.iso,media=cdrom,if=ide \
  -drive file=/app/alpine-disk.qcow2,if=virtio \
  -netdev user,id=n0,hostfwd=tcp::2222-:22 \
  -device virtio-net-pci,netdev=n0 \
  -nographic \
  -monitor none \
  -serial tcp:127.0.0.1:4321,server,nowait

input.command:
cd /tmp && nohup sh /tmp/boot.sh > /tmp/qemu.log 2>&1 &
echo "started pid $!"; sleep 3; cat /tmp/qemu.log
